headlines.title: Supreme Court temporarily restores full access to abortion pill
headlines.description: The Supreme Court provisionally blocked a lower court decision that would have limited availability nationwide of the abortion pill mifepristone.

headlines.title: Who will own the Bernie Sanders lane in 2028?
headlines.description: The massive grassroots movement that twice rallied behind Bernie Sanders' presidential bids is facing a big question: Who will emerge as its next leader?

headlines.title: Man charged in Trump assassination plot told FBI he didn't think he'd survive, feds say
headlines.description: A federal magistrate judge pressed the government over how alleged White House Correspondents Dinner shooter Cole Allen is being treated behind bars.

headlines.title: Rudy Giuliani is recovering from pneumonia and still 'critical but stable,' spokesman says
headlines.description: Rudy Giuliani was hospitalized with pneumonia and remains in critical but stable condition, his spokesperson, Ted Goodman, said Monday.“Mayor Rudy Giuliani is recovering from pneumonia and is being monitored as a precautionary measure," Goodman said in a statement, adding that the former New York City mayor had been diagnosed with restrictive airway disease in the wake of the Sept. 11 attacks

headlines.title: Supreme Court issues temporary pause on order blocking mail access to abortion pill
headlines.description: The Supreme Court has issued a temporary pause on a lower court ruling that restricted telehealth and mail access to the abortion pill mifepristone.

headlines.title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Critical but Stable Condition
headlines.description: Former New York City mayor Rudy Giuliani has been hospitalized in critical but stable condition, according to his spokesperson. The spokesperson did not specify what led to the hospitalization.

headlines.title: Trump Says US Navy Will Escort Ships out of Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: President Donald Trump has announced that the United States military will help guide stranded ships out of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran is responding with new threats, warning that if any foreign armed force approaches or enters the critical passage, it “will be subjected to attack.” NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Anger and despair among Jewish Britons after spate of attacks
headlines.description: LONDON — Britain in 2026 is a place where Jack Hur doesn’t feel safe wearing his Star of David pendant.

headlines.title: How the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner chaos unfolded
headlines.description: Secret Service faced challenges evacuating President, Vice President, Cabinet officials and Congressional leaders after gunman rushes D.C. gala.

headlines.title: Trump says U.S. will begin guiding ships through Strait of Hormuz
headlines.description: President Donald Trump said Sunday that the U.S. will guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz, as he has offered little optimism that he will accept a recent proposal from Iran to end the war

headlines.title: White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting suspect no longer on suicide watch, lawyers say
headlines.description: Defense lawyers for the man charged with trying to assassinate President Donald Trump during the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner last week say he’s being deprived of his dignity and resources by being unnecessarily kept under suicide precaution status.

headlines.title: Acting AG says indictment against James Comey goes beyond seashell photo
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said Sunday that the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey goes beyond the Instagram post central to his case.“This is not just about a single Instagram post,” Blanche told NBC News’ “Meet the Press” on Sunday.
